data.title: Why a PHP reader (and Laravel compatibility)?
data.excerpt: PHP runs 75% of the web. Laravel is the dominant modern PHP framework. Here's why @webhouse/cms ships a first-class PHP reader.
data.content:
## The PHP audience

PHP runs 75% of websites globally — WordPress alone powers 43% of the web. Laravel is the dominant modern PHP framework, used by Disney, BBC, and TripAdvisor for production apps. There are millions of PHP developers and they need a content layer that doesn't fight them.

## Why a reader, not an API?

A file reader is just `glob()` and `json_decode()`. It's instant. It works offline. It's testable with PHPUnit fixtures. And it's a pattern PHP developers already know from Laravel's `File` facade.

## What's in the package

- `Webhouse\Cms\Reader::collection(name, locale)` — list published documents
- `Webhouse\Cms\Reader::document(collection, slug)` — load one document
- `Webhouse\Cms\Reader::findTranslation(doc, collection)` — sibling via translationGroup
- `Webhouse\Cms\Reader::globals()` — cached singleton
- `Webhouse\Cms\Reader::string($doc, $key, $default)` — safe field accessor
- Path traversal protection via `SAFE_NAME` regex + `realpath()` prefix check

~140 lines of PHP. Single dependency: `league/commonmark` for markdown rendering (optional).

## Laravel integration

The `Reader` class works in any Laravel project. Drop the file in `app/Services/Webhouse/`, register as a singleton in your service provider, and inject it into controllers. No facades, no service location — just modern PHP DI.
data.author: Christian Broberg
